page.content: The data presented in the table provides a comprehensive overview of the characteristics and methodologies employed in an open-label trial investigating the efficacy of an extended-release methylphenidate preparation for Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD).

page.content:
The study includes a diverse cohort of minors, with a mean age of 13.3 years (SD = 2.4), and a notable sex distribution of 7 boys, highlighting the gender prevalence observed in ADHD diagnoses. Additionally, the social status of the parents has been documented, which may play a critical role in understanding the environmental influences on the study population. 

The trial also emphasizes the technical aspects of genomic analysis, as evidenced by metrics such as the input amount of DNA (1000 ng) and the coverage levels achieved during library preparation, utilizing the KAPA LTP kit from Kapa Biosystems. These methodological details are essential for assessing the reliability and validity of the genetic findings related to the AANAT1 gene, which has been implicated in ADHD-related behaviors. Overall, this table serves as a foundational reference for interpreting the trial's outcomes in relation to ADHD treatment.
